title: indigofera cavallii
intro: Indigofera cavallii is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses. The leaves and roots of the plant are used in herbal medicine, and it is often referred to as "tawa-tawa" due to its ability to repel certain insects. The medicinal properties of Indigofera cavallii include its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
